rulings.Case Title: MARK SOLE et al VS. MEDCAP MANAGEMENT & RESEARCH LLC, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Lift Stay Pending Arbitration And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARK SOLE, ADINA SAFER, MARK SOLE AND ADINA LYNN SAFER, Motion To Lift Stay Pending Arbitration And Request For Sanctions. Denied. "[A] trial court may not lift a stay of litigation merely because a party cannot afford the costs associated with arbitration." (MKJA, Inc. v. 123 Fit Franchising, LLC (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 643, 660.) Plaintiffs' arguments regarding delay and insufficient discovery responses must be raised to the arbitrator. "The arbitrator, and not the court, decides questions of procedure and discovery." (Titan Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 488.) Further, "it is also up to the arbitrator, not the court, to grant relief for delay in bringing the arbitration to a resolution." (Id.) JAMS rules provide that a plaintiff may advance the arbitration fees so that the arbitration may proceed. If the plaintiff prevails, the fees are added to the award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN HOPKINS VS. SALVATION ARMY et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication. The motion is granted. The release/waiver of liability bars this action. In addition, plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement of disputed facts and that is grounds alone to grant the motion. See CCP 437c(b)(3). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN INTEGRATED SERVICES, INC.,A CALIFORNIA VS. VERTIMAX VENTURES, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting In Part And Denying In Part Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT VERTIMAX VENTURES, INC., JOHN CHEN, ROGERS AVE, LLC Motion To Set Aside Order Granting In Part And Denying In Part Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Denied. Cross-Defendants have not established abandonment, or a "de facto severance of the attorney client relationship." (Carroll v. Abbott Laboratories (1982) 32 Cal.3d 892, 901.) The evidence establishes, as summarized by Cross-Complainant in its opposition brief at pp. 2-6, that Cross-Defendants' counsel was actively involved in litigating the case prior to substituting out as counsel. Even assuming that Cross-Defendants established that their attorney "grossly mishandled" the discovery matters, this is not a basis for CCP sec. 473(b) relief. (Id. at 900.) Rather, absent abandonment, "[t]he client's redress for inexcusable neglect by counsel is¿an action for malpractice." (Id. at 898.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS VS. TAYLOR K. SMITH MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE, TAYLOR SMITH, M.D. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The court grants summary adjudication on the battery cause of action as unopposed. The court grants summary adjudication on the Elder Abuse cause of action because plaintiff cannot establish her prima facie claim. "[N]eglect within the meaning of Welfare and Institutions Code section 15610.57 covers an area of misconduct distinct from professional negligence. As used in the Act, neglect refers not to the substandard performance of medical services but, rather, to the failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs and comforts of elderly or dependent adults, regardless of their professional standing, to carry out their custodial obligations. Thus, the statutory definition of neglect speaks not of the undertaking of medical services, but of the failure to provide medical care." (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783 [original italics, internal citations omitted]; see also Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, 31; Worsham v. O'Connor Hospital (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 331.) In this case, defendants' facts are largely unopposed and show that defendants were providing medical care to plaintiff and did not deprive plaintiff of custodial services necessary to her well being. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBIN LEAMY et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ROBIN LEAMY, KRIS LEAMY Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. The court grants the motion for leave to amend. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and the proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case. The court will order the motion for summary judgment off calendar once the plaintiff files the second amendment complaint. The second amended complaint will render the motion for summary judgment moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. The court continues the current trial date to avoid prejudice to defendant. Defendant should have a right to attack the second amended complaint by demurrer, motion to strike and/or refile a motion for summary judgment. The court continues the trial date to December 7, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF WILDA WHITE Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories; And For Sanctions. Denied. The underlying request for admission addresses a matter on which expert opinion is required: the standard of care to be observed by Defendant in treatment of Plaintiff. The accompanying Form Interrogatory 17.1 seeks the facts and documents which support the Defendant's denial of the request and therefore delves into matters for which expert testimony is required. Under County of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1446, 1455-56, questions can be posed to a defendant physician in their capacity as a percipient witness. However, a physician who is the subject of the malpractice action is not required to provide opinion testimony unless the physician is designated as an expert witness. Here, no experts have been declared and Defendant is responding to the interrogatory in a non-expert capacity. Because the underlying request for admission necessarily implicates expert testimony and not facts or details of treatment, the objection to the interrogatory is appropriate. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT VARENA VS. JOHN CHRISTOPHER DUBA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Requests For Admission (Genuineness Of Documents) - Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT SCOTT VARENA's Motion To Compel Responses To Cross-Defendants Requests For Admission (Genuineness Of Documents) - Set One. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The Court finds that moving party Varena filed this and the other accompanying discovery motions even after substantial, verified responses had been served by defendant/cross-complainant Duba, and that Mr. Varena's counsel Ilson New did not make a reasonable effort to meet and confer to resolve the perceived discovery disputes. In addition, the moving party has failed to comply with CRC 3.1345(c), which requires that for each discovery request, there must be "a statement of the factual and legal reasons for compelling further responses, answers, or production as to each matter in dispute." The moving papers reiterated the discovery requests and responses as required, but then at the end provided an omnibus statement of legal and factual reasons for compelling further responses to the requests- there was no individualized argument for further response as to each request. This failure is inadequate under CRC 3.1145(c), and is by itself grounds for denial of the motion. Sanctions are awarded against Scott Varena and counsel Ilson New in the total amount of $6,331.60 (for all 10 motions), payable to Duba's counsel Cox, Wootton et al within 10 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS F MCCORMICK VS. SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Thomas Mccormick'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 4, Plaintiff Thomas Mccormick's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two. (Part 2 of 2.) 17, 18: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present 19 (screenshots fo searches) Parties to meet and confer and discuss resolution. Set 2 22 This demand is overbroad. SM shall produce WRITINGS relating to the plaintiff's policy, and all contracts/agreements relating to SM's relationship with Swiss RE vis a vis sale or reinsurance of individual policies 23 Overbroad. These appear to be deposition questions. 24 Overbroad. SM shall produce WRITINGS discussing reasons why Swiss Re bought or reinsured SM's disability line of business. 25 Overbroad. SM shall produce WRITINGS relating to the sale or reinsurance of plaintiff's policy. 26, 27: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present. 28 Overbroad and may seek irrelevant and/or private financial data. Plaintiff should be prepared to discuss (including legal authorities, if any) why discovery of financial arrangements between SM and Swiss Re would be relevant or lead to discovery of admissible evidence in this case. 29, 30 31 (comms re SM and Swiss Re Csc re handling individual claims) SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present, limited to general practices (not each individual claim) and to plaintiff's claim 32-35: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present 36, 37: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present, limited to disability policies 38-40: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present 41 parties to be prepared to discuss scope of documents sought and why they should or should not be produced. Security Mutual (SM) shall for each demand serve a verified response stating that it will produce all responsive non-privileged documents it its possession, custody or control, except as said demand has been modified by the parties or court order. SM shall prepare a privilege log for all docs withheld, stating at minimum: 1) Bates # 2) date 3) type of document (e.g., letter, memo, email) 4) number of pages 5) preparer 6) recipient 7) general subject matter 8) legal grounds for withholding. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS F MCCORMICK VS. SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF THOMAS MCCORMICK's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Documents Responsive To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Parties are ordered to further meet and confer and attempt to narrow or eliminate issues so as to minimize the number of demands for production that need to be discussed at the hearing. Parties shall each bring a proposed order. By way of guidance, the Court offers its tentative views on the following document demands: RPD 3 (claim file) Security Mutual (SM) shall produce all WRITINGS ( as defined in Evid. Code sec. 250) indicating who has handled the claim. 4, 5: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present 6: SM shall produce the complete operations manual(s) from 2010 to present, as well as all writings addressing claims handling practices and procedures. The trade secrets objections did not satisfy the format requirements. If there are trade secret or confidentiality concerns, the parties shall be prepared to enter into a stipulated protective order. 9, 10: SM shall produce all responsive WRITINGS from 2010 to present 11, 12, 13: This seems more appropriate for a contention interrogatory. If plaintiff has the entire claim file, he should have all documents he needs for this demand. (Continued to Part 2 of 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FLORENCE GASPER VS. SUTTER HEALTH,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 11, PLAINTIFF FLORENCE GASPER Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file her proposed Second Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. WILLIAM HILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 17, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's Motion For Order Establishing Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
